# Figure description: Gamma VII.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma VII.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section (профиль)** — specifically a combined gravity/magnetic anomaly profile with an interpreted geological section beneath it. It is designated **Профиль VIII** (Profile VIII). The figure contains two main panels: an upper panel showing geophysical curves plotted against a horizontal distance axis, and a lower panel showing the interpreted subsurface geological structure along the same profile line.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Profile designation:** Профиль VIII (Profile VIII)
- **Map sheet reference:** The upper right corner references **черт. № 110, лист 3** (drawing No. 110, sheet 3), and the stamp reads **36572** — confirming this belongs to Report 36572
- **Year:** 1984 (visible in upper left: "1984 г.")
- **Geographic/survey context:** Northern Kazakhstan regional survey (M-42 / N-42 map sheets, 1:200,000)
- **Horizontal extent:** The profile extends approximately **0 to ~900–1000 km** in profile distance units (likely in meters or hundreds of meters; tick marks visible at approximately 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 units along the x-axis)
- **Vertical scale (upper panel):** Anomaly values range from approximately **−2 to +32** units (likely milligals for gravity or nanoTeslas/gammas for magnetics), with a zero baseline clearly marked
- **Depth scale (lower panel):** Extends from surface down to approximately **−10 to −12 km** depth

---

## Key Content

### Upper Panel (Geophysical Anomaly Curves):
- A **prominent, sharply rising anomaly** is visible in the left-centre portion of the profile, reaching a peak of approximately **+32 units** — this represents a major positive anomaly, likely a Bouguer gravity high or magnetic high, associated with a dense or magnetic subsurface body
- The curve then descends steeply and plateaus at moderate positive values (~+4 to +8 units) across the right half of the profile
- Several **secondary undulations** are present in the middle and right sections of the profile, including subsidiary peaks at approximately stations 3, 4, and near the right terminus
- A **zero reference line** is drawn horizontally across the panel
- A **negative anomaly zone** (below zero) appears near the left margin (approximately −1 to −2 units), suggesting a less dense or non-magnetic surface unit at the profile start
- Points along the profile are numbered (visible labels: **①, ②, ③, ④** etc. — borehole or control point locations intersecting the profile)

### Lower Panel (Interpreted Geological Section):
- The section is divided into **horizontal stratigraphic/structural layers** defined by dashed and solid lines
- **Rock units** are labelled with formation symbols; visible annotations include:
  - **γQ₃** or similar (Quaternary sediments/cover)
  - **PZ₂β** or similar (Palaeozoic basement, possibly with intrusive or volcanic components)
  - **PE₂β** (likely Riphean or Proterozoic units)
  - **PZ, γr** or similar (granitic rocks within the Palaeozoic section)
- **Density/susceptibility values** are annotated within each block, in the format **± X.XX г/см³** (grams per cubic centimetre) or **× 10⁻⁵ СИ** (SI susceptibility units):
  - Values visible include: **+0.05 г/см³**, **+0.03 г/см³**, **−0.02 г/см³**, **−0.03 г/см³**, **+0.16 г/см³**, **+0.10 г/см³**, **+0.18 г/см³** (approximate readings — these are density contrasts relative to a reference density)
- Vertical boundaries (faults or contact zones) are shown as near-vertical dashed lines, dividing the section into **5–6 structural blocks**
- The deepest portion of the section (lower sub-panel, extending to ~−30 to −35 km depth based on the continuation of the profile line downward off the main section) shows a **steeply plunging feature** — likely a deep crustal root or intrusive body extending well below normal crustal section depths, consistent with a major gravity high source body
